creator: uzsolt@FreeBSD.org
text:
(In reply to Mikhail T. from comment #11)
Yes, there are most important things than this comment vs. ECHO_MSG.

IMHO your comments (their reason) are ambiguous. I thought you want comment trivial things and I wrote they are unnecessary (eg. "Installing documentation tree..." - next row after "post-install-DOCS-on:"). You wrote they're messages to you while building.

I think if I misunderstood then somebody else can misunderstood too.

I think if you want messages while building should use ECHO_MSG because it's the standard way. Another reason: if later ECHO_MSG will change (for example print the message in color red/yellow/green (bikeshed :) ), or redirect to a specific file), will not be effective for your version.
